message:
test(readme): Scalar inspection + semantic comparison methods

Pins frankenpandas-fd90.56: Scalar has nine
inspection/comparison methods documented as pandas-parity
surfaces (semantic_eq, semantic_le, semantic_ge, semantic_cmp,
coalesce, to_f64, is_missing, is_na, is_null, is_nan), but
prior to this commit only is_missing was exercised by an
integration test. The remainder were silently regressable.

The new readme_scalar_inspection_methods case asserts:

* is_missing / is_na / is_null / is_nan on the three null
  variants (NullKind::NaN, NullKind::NaT, NullKind::Null)
  plus a non-null Int64 baseline. is_nan distinguishes NaN
  from NaT/generic Null.
* coalesce(non_null, _) returns the first; coalesce(null,
  fallback) returns the fallback — covers both NaN and Null
  source values.
* semantic_eq is reflexive on NaN (unlike PartialEq, which is
  false for NaN.eq(NaN)) and matches PartialEq elsewhere.
* semantic_le / semantic_ge are weakly ordered on equal
  values and behave correctly on strict-ordered pairs.
* semantic_cmp returns Less / Greater / Equal for ordered
  Int64 inputs.
* to_f64 succeeds for Int64 and Float64 (preserving value),
  promotes Bool → 1.0 / 0.0, and surfaces an Err for
  non-numeric variants (Utf8).

Beads: chore beads JSONL flush capturing fd90.56's transition
to in_progress against the same commit. The bead transitions to
closed on the next br sync after the final assertion lands.
